design_philosophy.core_loop: Select individual humans from a group, assign tools and positioning, manipulate them to overcome environmental puzzles and reach the exit tile while managing tribe population as a resource
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing human casualties against level progression; risk of tribe depletion creating game-over state vs. need to solve complex multi-step puzzles
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a prehistoric tribe through evolution-themed challenges using emergent tool mechanics and cooperative human stacking/positioning
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately inspired by Lemmings but with intentional differentiation: bigger graphics, cartoon aesthetics (Tom and Jerry style), more varied level design, and individual human control rather than swarm manipulation. Tool-centric puzzle design where objects (spears, torches, wheels, ropes, witch doctor) create solution vectors rather than player attributes

difficulty.lives_system: Tribe population system: up to 8 humans per level with variable minimums. Human deaths are permanent losses; replacement comes only from tribal reserve. Game over if tribe falls below level minimum
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows jumping to any previously reached level from game start, enabling mid-game progression but no in-level checkpointing
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based structure with passwords for external saves; no mid-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player has significant agency in risk/reward: can attempt dangerous sequences with expendable humans or pursue safer routes at cost of time. Tool selection and sequencing creates puzzle variety but no explicit difficulty settings documented

progression.upgrade_system: Asymmetric progression: humans are unlocked/rescued during levels and added to permanent tribe pool. No traditional leveling; progression measured by tribe size accumulation and access to new tools per level
progression.unlock_mechanism: Tools introduced per-level (spear in level 1, later witch doctor, wheels, ropes, torches). Each level presents independent goal (reach exit, kill dinosaurs, find tool, rescue humans) with its own tool subset and human count constraints
replayability_hooks: Tribe size management creates different solutions on replay
Password system enables non-linear level access for sequence-breaking
Tool availability variation per level encourages creative reuse
Enemy tribe members and dinosaur encounters add dynamic hazards

world_building_techniques: Prehistoric environment with dinosaurs and enemy tribes
Colored exit tiles and tribal NPCs (pterodactylus) as world flavor

faction_indicators: Enemy tribe members as hostile NPCs with spears
Player tribe identity through accumulated humans
danger_signals: Dinosaurs that hunt unarmed humans within walking range
Resource scarcity: limited humans per level forcing risk assessment
environmental_storytelling: Pterodactyl rideable creatures suggesting symbiosis potential
Tool progression reflecting human technological advancement
Tribal gathering/rescue sequences implying social bonds
